title: E & A Occupancy + Fire Sprinkler Information (Eagle Pass City Council)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: E & A Occupancy + Fire Sprinkler Information (Eagle Pass City Council)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request an electronic, parcel-level extract containing current Group E (Educational) and Group A (Assembly) occupancies and associated fire-sprinkler information for all parcels within the jurisdiction: 

Please provide one CSV with the following columns (your native field names are fine):
- Parcel identifier (APN / PIN / BBL)
• Site address (street #, street, city, ZIP)
• Latitude & longitude
• Internal building / permit ID
• Year built
• Floor area (sq ft)
• Number of stories
• Current IBC occupancy code (E, A-1…A-5)
• Occupancy description
• Fire-sprinkler present (Yes / No / Unknown)
• Date of last fire-sprinkler inspection (if tracked)
• Owner name & mailing address (if stored)

If any field is not maintained, please note that rather than withholding the entire record.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

David Cavise

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Pennsylvania Department of State)

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Pennsylvania Department of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: Deaths-in-custody

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Deaths-in-custody
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

The database or records of all Illinois law enforcement agencies deaths-in-custody up to the date this request is ultimately fulfilled, including all deaths-in-custody reported pursuant both Illinois state legislation (730 ILCS 210/3-5) and federal legislation (34 U.S.C. § 60105), as well as all data dictionaries, database scheme documentation or other documentation on the collection and organizatin of the data. 

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

title: 2026 Ohio (Office of the Attorney General - Georgia)

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: 2026 Ohio (Office of the Attorney General - Georgia)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:
Pursuant to the Ohio Freedom of Information Law and the new 2026 Ohio Sunshine Laws Manual I hereby request the following records:
Pursuant to Section 149.43 of the Ohio Revised Code, I hereby request the following records:  unofficial copies of any documents of complaints and actions taken (any info allowable by law) filed with the Ohio Charitable Law and the Ohio Secretary of State regarding :


Congregation Sholom
DBA: Temple Sholom of Cincinnati (doing business as that has lapsed)
10828 Kenwood Rd. Cincinnati, OH 45242
EIN 31-0584318


Central Conference of American Rabbis - OH Registration #26991
EIN: 13-1769747
NY State Registration Number - 05-99-38
Recently changed address:
183 Madison Ave #1504
New York, NY 10016
Address listed with Charities Bureau:
355 LEXINGTON AVE 8th Floor, null
NEW YORK , NY 10017

Ohio Subsidiary
Ohio Entity ID 26991
1160 Dublin Road, Suite 400
Columbus OH 43215
Pursuant to Section 149.43 of the Ohio Revised Code & New York Public Officers Law § 87(2), I request access to and copies of all non-exempt public records in the possession, custody, or control of the Ohio Attorney General or any other public office relating to the Central Conference of American Rabbis & Temple Sholom (Cincinnati, Ohio), including but not limited to records connected to any complaints, investigations, or actions taken under Ohio Charitable Law.
If any portion of the requested records is withheld, I request that all reasonably segregable, non-exempt portions be provided, with redactions. 
This request specifically includes, and is not limited to:
All copies of any and all organizational and governing documents submitted to, filed with, or otherwise in the possession of any public office relating to Congregation Sholom (Temple Sholom of Cincinnati) and the Central Conference of American Rabbis, including but not limited to articles of incorporation or association, bylaws, constitutions, amendments, and any other governance or structural documents.
All complaints submitted against Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis and its representatives, attorneys, or agents, submitted to any public office, including responses to complaints, position statements, and supporting exhibits.
All documents, correspondence, and materials submitted by Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis and its representatives, attorneys, or agents to any public office, including responses to complaints, position statements, and supporting exhibits
All communications between Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis  (or its representatives) and any public office, including emails, letters, requests for information, follow-up communications, and meeting summaries.
All documents, records, or materials that were reviewed, relied upon, cited, or considered by any public office in connection with complaints, inquiries, or investigations involving Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis regardless of whether such materials originated from Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis or third parties.
All records created by any public office relating to Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis including but not limited to investigative summaries, factual findings, memoranda, recommendations, determinations, closure letters, or case status communications.
Any financial, contractual, or funding-related records involving Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis that were submitted to or reviewed by a public office, including any materials referencing grants, loans, or other funding (including but not limited to COVID-related funding) if such materials were part of any complaint, review, or investigation.
All complaints and supporting documentation submitted by third parties relating to Temple Sholom & Central Conference of American Rabbis including any attachments or exhibits.
All final or closed-case materials, sanctions, settlements, assurance of discontinuance agreements, administrative subpoenas, court filings, court orders, closure letters and determination summaries.
Any Foia Requests made to the Attorney General for information on Temple Sholom or Central Conference of American Rabbis, the date of the request, the name of the person making the request, and the responsive documents provided.
I request that I be properly classified as a news media requester. I am a complainant and a writer for the Times of Israel and I am requesting this information in that editorial capacity.  The Times of Israel is widely recognized for its reporting, publishing regular features on issues of public interest and importance.  The information may also be used in a documentary that is currently being filed.  You may view my articles at the address listed:  https://blogs.timesofisrael.com/author/julie-guilfoyle/
If you have any questions about my media status, please contact me.
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request be filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.  If there is a large cost, then as an alternative, I request for the records to be made available for inspection.
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request.
Sincerely,
Julie Guilfoyle

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Julie Guilfoyle

title: Department of Defense support for TV miniseries "Ice Airport Alaska"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of Defense support for TV miniseries "Ice Airport Alaska"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any documents pertaining to collaboration between the Department of Defense (DoD) and the production of the TV miniseries "Ice Airport Alaska" (2020-2023). Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; and all records of communications between employees of DoD and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Ice Airport Alaska," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"Ice Airport Alaska" appears on a list of DoD entertainment industry collaborations released to me through FOIA. The list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Clark County Court Administrator of the District Court Jury List Request

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Clark County Court Administrator of the District Court Jury List Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to NV Rev Stat § 6.045(5)(c)(1)

The following materials regarding the compilation and composition of the state’s master jury list:

All current policies and procedures regarding how the current master jury list was compiled and how often master jury lists are reviewed and updated. Please name all source lists (driver’s license records, voter registration lists, etc) that were utilized in creating the current master jury list.

All reports, databases, spreadsheets, or equivalent materials regarding the composition of the current master jury list. Please include the total number of individuals on the current master jury list and all relevant demographic data, including but not limited to the race, age and gender of individuals that comprise the current master jury list.

In the event that there are any fees for obtaining or copying these records, please inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. Should the fees exceed $100, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees as I am requesting the information on behalf of a 501(c)(3) nonprofit and the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how master jury lists are created. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes.
If possible, please provide the requested documents electronically. However, if this is not feasible, please inform me of the available methods for obtaining these records. Further, if you expect a significant delay in responding to this request, please contact me with information concerning when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.
If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption that justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within the timeframe required by the statute.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Elijah Salisbury

communications.communication:
Thank you so much for everything you've sent over. 

I was wondering if there was any explanation for some of the missing race data. The "Unknown" column is marked "Yes" for a plethora of potential jurors across the shared data (about 44% across the shared years). Is this due to participants not filling this portion out during the summons process or another occurrence?

Looking forward to hearing from you soon.

Thank you in advance, 
Elijah

communications.subject: Record Request Ticket #12351
communications.communication:
Good morning.  I am responding to your follow-up question regarding the "unknown" column in the jury report that was provided to you.  I have confirmed that the "Yes" designations in those fields denote when a prospective juror declines to make a selection.

Please do not hesitate to reach out if I can be of further assistance.

[Signature]
This email may contain legally privileged and/or confidential information. If you are not the intended recipient(s), any dissemination, distribution or copying of this email message is strictly prohibited. If you have received this message in error, please immediately notify the sender and delete this email from your computer.

title: Prisacts

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Prisacts
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records captioned "Prisacts" or "Revolutionary Activities in Prisons", including file 62-HQ-116018 and any bulky files, EBFs, or sub files if they exist.

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies. This is also to include any responsive records held in offsite locations, such as the Alexandria Records Center. Please include both investigatory and non-investigatory records in your search. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Paul Galante

Please be advised that efoia@subscriptions.fbi.gov is a no-reply email
address. Questions regarding your FOIA request may be directed to
foipaquestions@fbi.gov. If you have received a FOIPA request number, please
include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

**

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBI’s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBI’s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the ‘Check Status of Your
FOI/PA Request tool’ link. Status updates are performed on a weekly basis. If
you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Purchasing Policy
communications.communication:
Hello,

The Town's Purchasing Policy is not a criminal justice record and should be provided by CORA (not CCJRA), and the first hour of retrieval is free. 

Best practices and public transparency would have this policy on the Town's website. The County, the City of Delta, the library district and even the Town of Paonia have theirs freely available on their websites, such that a CORA request is not necessary. It would be nice to see Hotchkiss do the same. 

Please fulfill this request. 

Thank you.

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Purchasing Policy
communications.communication:
Hello,

It has been a week. Am I understanding correctly that the Town of Hotchkiss is not willing to freely provide its purchasing policy as per CORA? 

This is NOT a criminal justice record and should be provided as per CORA, and the first hour of retrieval is free. 

Again, best practices and public transparency would have this policy on the Town's website. The County, the City of Delta, the library district and even the Town of Paonia have theirs freely available on their websites, such that a CORA request is not necessary. 

It would be nice to see Hotchkiss do the same.

Please fulfill this request without further delay.

Thank you.

communications.subject: [OpenRecords] Request FOIL-2024-002-00272 Submitted to Mayor's Office (OOM)
communications.communication:
Your request FOIL-2024-002-00272 has been successfully submitted to the Mayor's Office (OOM).
                                The details of your request are shown below. 
 
Request Title: De Blasio Administration Fair Workweek Emails 
 
Request Description: To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

All emails and other records released in response to FOIL requests 2016-002-00410 or 2017-002-00582.

Note that the openrecords.nyc.gov website states that documents responsive to these requests "have been posted on the OpenRecords portal." However, no files are publicly available. A description of the records can be found on the following webpages:
https://www.politico.com/states/new-york/albany/story/2018/02/01/powerful-union-drafted-edited-fast-food-worker-bills-228603
https://a860-openrecords.nyc.gov/request/view/FOIL-2016-002-00410
https://a860-openrecords.nyc.gov/request/view/FOIL-2017-002-00582

This request is being made for non-commercial research purposes and the requested documents will be made available to the general public.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer that the request be filled electronically by e-mail attachment if possible.

Thank you for your assistance with this request. I look forward to receiving your response within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Peter Fugiel
Postdoctoral Research Fellow
Rutgers School of Management & Labor Relations

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request #FOIL-2024-002-00272
communications.communication:
Dear Records Appeal Officer,

Two years ago, I submitted a request (FOIL-2024-002-00272) for emails and other records pertaining to the New York City Fair Workweek Law. These records were previously requested in 2016 and 2017 (FOIL-2016-002-00410, FOIL-2017-002-00582) and subsequently posted to the OpenRecords portal, according to the closing messages dated 12/26/2017. For reasons that are still unclear to me, none of these records are publicly available on the so-called OpenRecords portal. 

After seven missed deadlines and extensions, I received a letter (see attached) from Records Access Officer Megha Jain on March 19, 2026, informing me that the Mayor’s Office identified records responsive to my request. The letter states that "The responsive records are attached." However, the letter is a single-page PDF with no attachments. I followed the instructions in the accompanying email (from: donotreply@records.nyc.gov, to: 160724-04060059@requests.muckrock.com), logging into the OpenRecords account associated with this request. I see no records or attachments, not even the response letter, only the generic acknowledgement and extension notices. 

I am appealing the constructive denial of my request through the excessive delay and failure of the Mayor's Office to provide any responsive records. Note that I am using the MuckRock website to track this request and ensure that any records released are publicly accessible at the following webpage: https://www.muckrock.com/foi/new-york-city-17/previously-released-fair-workweek-emails-160724. Please respond in writing (to 160724-04060059@requests.muckrock.com) within ten business days of your receipt of my appeal, stating whether the request is granted or denied in full or in part. If the appeal is granted, please state a date certain by which the records requested will be produced. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter,
Peter Fugiel

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Chautauqua County Sheriff)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Chautauqua County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: FW: CCSO Use of Force Records-Sinyangwe, Samuel
communications.communication:
S. Sinyangwe:

We have worked with the Chautauqua County Sheriff's Office to find the report it had responsive to your FOIL request. Please note that some of the information has been redacted from the report pursuant to Public Officers Law §89 to prevent an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy.

As a result of our inquiries, we have determined there are additional files, for open investigations, that are relevant to the parameters of your FOIL request. However, we must deny the release of any of those records under Public Officers Law 87(2)(e). These are records compiled for law enforcement purposes, and disclosure would interfere with the ongoing judicial proceedings.

Please be advised that the Chautauqua County Jail has informed us that they did not track use of force incident categories until 2021.

Due to the file size, I will be sharing the file with you shortly through OneDrive so that you may review and download the file.

Thank you.

Chautauqua County Office of the County Attorney
Gerace Office Building
3 North Erie St.
Mayville, NY 14757
(716) 753-4247
Fax: (716) 753-4129

title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Radford University)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Radford University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick.

communications.subject: Mail to kcasteel@radford.edu undeliverable

communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Nash and Mr. Chick,

The information you requested in your email of March 24, 2026,  is attached and was redacted pursuant to Code of VA § 2.2-3704(B)(2). The requested records are being provided in part and are being withheld in part because the release of part of the records may be withheld under the Code of VA. Pursuant to Code of VA § 2.2-3705.1(1) and (10) the attached documents have been redacted to exclude all personnel and personal contact information:

title: Parental Objections (Laconia School District/SAU30)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Parental Objections (Laconia School District/SAU30)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

All parent and/or guardian objections to instructional and library materials from this school district between the 2023-2024 and 2024-2025 school years.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: ww2 reports request

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: ww2 reports request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act. I am requesting access to and copies of the following historical intelligence reports regarding German underground installations, factories, and assembly plants during World War II.

These reports were generated by the Combined Intelligence Objectives Sub-committee (CIOS), the British Intelligence Objectives Sub-committee (BIOS), and the Joint Intelligence Objectives Agency (JIOA).
Requested Documents:

1. CIOS File Numbers:

    File No. II-12: Visit to Underground V-1 Manufacturing Plant (Sept 1944)

    File No. X-1: Caves at Bad Maries, used for Storage of Peroxide (Col. W.H. Wheeler)

    File No. XXIV-26, 27: Electronic Valve (Tube) Factories TeKaDe, Helmbrechts and Philips; Minden

    File No. XXVI-44: Messerschmidt Bombproof Assembly Plant Landsberg

    File No. XXVI-87: Dachs-I (Underground Lubricating Oil Plant) Porta, Westphalia

    File No. XXVII-59: Underground German Liquid Oxygen Plant, Wittring

    File No. XXVIII-45: Investigation of Group 2 Targets in Nordhausen Area

    File No. XXXII-15: Report on Visit to Kuckuck I Plant at Niedersachswerfen

    File No. XXXII-17: Underground Factories in Central Germany (Col. W.R.J. Cook)

2. CIOS Evaluation Reports:

    Evaluation Report No. 87: Investigation of Dannenberg V-1 Assembly Plant

    Evaluation Report No. 106: Target No. 28/5.55 Industriekontor, G.m.b.H.

    Evaluation Report No. 182: Underground Factory Sites in Ansbach

    Evaluation Report No. 238: Hauptausschuss Elektrotechnik (List of Underground Plants)

    Evaluation Report No. 252: Phillips Underground Valve Plant, Porta, Westfalia

3. JIOA Final Reports:

    Final Report No. 1: German Underground Installations. Part One: Unique Design and Construction

    Final Report No. 2: German Underground Installations. Part Two: Adaptations of Existing Facilities

    Final Report No. 3: German Underground Installations. Part Three: Various Installations

4. BIOS Final Reports:

    Final Report No. 65: Philips Valvo Works, Hamburg; Hammerwerke, Minden; C.H. Müller A.G., Hamburg

    Final Report No. 324: Band Oil Refinery at Porta (Nr. Minden)

Fee Waiver / Categorization

I am requesting these for personal research and historical study. I am willing to pay fees up to $[Insert Amount, e.g., $25.00], but please notify me if the costs will exceed this amount. Given the age of these documents (80+ years), I request that they be provided in digital format if available to minimize costs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tino Von Struckmann

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request: ww2 reports request
communications.communication:
This is an automated reply to inform you that the National Archives and Records Administration, Transparency and Access Program (FOIA Program Area) has received your email.
We will respond within 20 working days. Please note that submitting duplicate requests may increase wait times. If you have any questions, you may call our office at 301-837-3190.

You can also visit our web page at www.archives.gov/research/foia.

communications.subject: Re: [26-31772] Freedom of Information Act Request - Tino Von Struckmann
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Struckmann:



This is in response to your Freedom of Information Act request for
"historical intelligence reports regarding German underground
installations, factories, and assembly plants during World War II." Your
request was received in this office on March 4, 2026, with the assigned
control number 26-31772



*As these records are not classified, they do not require a FOIA request
and are available for you or your representative to review for pertinent
records.* We make reasonable efforts to assist researchers with their work.
As a matter of policy, the National Archives is staffed to assist
researchers with their work, including but not limited to pointing
researchers to helpful finding aids to begin their research. However, given
the limitations on staff resources, we do not generally conduct substantive
research in response to requests filed under the Freedom of Information
Act, except in circumstances where documents are easily locatable.



British Intelligence Objectives Sub-committee (BIOS), Combined Intelligence
Objectives Sub-committee (CIOS) and Joint Intelligence Objectives Agency
(JIOA) reports are not always filed by report number nor are the always
found in distinct record series of such reports. They can also be found
filed under report titles and/or by topic under broader subject headings.
BIOS, CIOS and JIOA reports can be found among the following record series:



Records of the War Department General and Special Staffs (Record Group
165), Publication Files (Entry NM84 79
<https://catalog.archives.gov/id/1557240>);



Records of the Office of Scientific Research and Development (Record Group
227), Records of the London Mission, 1941-1946 (Entry NC138 176
<https://catalog.archives.gov/id/6920098>) (under CIO subseries, file
“evaluation reports”)



Records of the U.S. Strategic Bombing Survey (Record Group 243), Security
Classified  European Survey Intelligence Library, 1939-1945 (Entry I10 36
<https://catalog.archives.gov/id/561425>) (Envelope 881 contains target
priorities and reports prepared by BIOS during 1944-1945)



Records of the Army Staff (Record Group 319), Publication Files (Entry NM3
82 <https://catalog.archives.gov/id/656424>)



Records of Allied Operational and Occupation Headquarters, World War II
(Record Group 331), CIOS Reports, 1944-1945 (Entry UD 13-D
<https://catalog.archives.gov/id/573950>)



Records of Headquarters, European Theater of Operations, United States Army
(Record Group 498), Records Relating to CIOS, 1944-1945 (Entry UD 1161
<https://catalog.archives.gov/id/5898423>).



Among the Records of the Office of the Secretary of Defense (Record Group
330) are following record series which contain records of the Joint
Intelligence Objectives Agency (JIOA):



General Correspondence, 1947–1952 (Entry A1 1062-A
<https://catalog.archives.gov/id/7450323>)

Foreign Scientist Case Files, 1947–1958 (Entry A1 1-B
<https://catalog.archives.gov/id/7450328>)



The Imperial War Museum appears to have a significant collection of BIOS,
CIOS and JIOA reports among the records in its collection. A link to the
description of these records can be found here
<https://www.iwm.org.uk/collections/item/object/1030013221>.



If you have questions regarding the publicly available records recommended
above, you may contact us at archives2reference@nara.gov.  We hope you find
this information useful in conducting your research.



This concludes the processing of your request. We do not consider this a
denial of your FOIA request. If you are not satisfied with our action on
this request, your options for appeal and dispute resolution are attached.



Sincerely,



PAUL B. BROWN



Textual Reference Branch

National Archives at College Park, MD



Attachment

Dear Requester:  The City of Watsonville ("City") is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted. The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act ("PRA") (Government Code section 7920.000 et seq.) Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requestor makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. communications.communication:
City of Watsonville, CA ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-55: Dear Syd Jones, The City of Watsonville ("City") is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted. The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act ("PRA") (Government Code section 7290.000 et seq.) Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requestor makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The PRA provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to respond directly to questions or create new records, lists, or reports in response to a request. The City is typically afforded ten (10 days) from the date of receipt of your request to make a determination whether your request seeks disclosable public records. In accordance with the California Public Records Act, pursuant to Government Code section 7922.535, the City will require an extension of that deadline of up to an additional fourteen (14) days to make such a determination because of the reasons checked as follows: [ ] The need to search for and collect the requested records from facilities separate from the office processing the request. [X] The need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request. [ ] The need to compile data, to write programming language or a computer program, or to construct a computer report to extract data. Accordingly, the City will determine whether it possesses records responsive to your Request on or before 2/20/2026. If you have any questions regarding the City's response or would like to discuss your Request, please contact me at the number or email listed below. communications.subject: Your City of Watsonville, CA public records request #26-55 has been closed.
communications.communication:
City of Watsonville, CA ************************************************************************ Record request #26-55 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: Dear Syd Jones, The City of Watsonville (“City”) is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted, received on January 26, 2026 (“Request”).    The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act (“PRA”). (Government Code section 7920.000 et seq.) Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requestor makes a specific and focused request for the record, that record is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the record is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort.The PRA provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to respond directly to questions, requests for information, or create new records, lists, or reports in response to a request. On February 6, 2026, the City requested an additional 14 days to determine whether the City possessed records due to the need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request. On February 20, 2026, the City determined that it possesses records responsive to your Request. On March 13, 2026, the City provided the first batch of records responsive to your Request. The City now provides an additional batch of responsive, non-exempt records. The City redacted camera locations as the public interest in withholding clearly outweighs the public interest in disclosure to prevent camera tampering or vandalism and because the cameras are law enforcement investigatory tools. (Cal. Gov. Code section 7922.000.)                 With this letter and production, the City considers your Request closed.  If you have any questions regarding the City’s response or would like to discuss your Request, please contact us at cityclerk@watsonville.gov City Clerk's Office (831) 768-3010 275 Main Street, Suite 400, Watsonville, CA 95076 FAX: 831-761-0736 E-mail:  cityclerk@watsonville.gov Open Monday - Friday 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Watsonville, CA.

Technical support: See our help page

title: Flock Solar Panel - Property of NCIRC (Santa Cruz Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Solar Panel - Property of NCIRC (Santa Cruz Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

There is a solar panel powering a Flock ALPR camera located at Lat/Long 36.9677, -122.0186, the Northeast corner of 3rd Street and Riverside Ave in Santa Cruz, California. On that solar panel is a sticker that reads:
"Property of NCRIC / NCHIDTA",  "Flock Solar Panel", "10682". 

Please provide all contracts, MSAs, MOUs, NDAs, notes, emails and other documents associated with that solar panel.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Syd Jones

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Solar Panel - Property of NCIRC (Santa Cruz Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following California Public Records Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on Dec. 23, 2025. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: Public Records Response
communications.communication:
RESPONSE TO PUBLIC RECORDS ACT REQUEST – CITY CLERK
(CA. Gov’t Code §7920.000 et seq.)


Re: PUBLIC RECORDS ACT REQUEST RECEIVED ON ­Tuesday, December 23, 2025

The City of Santa Cruz responds to your public records request for information received on ­Tuesday, December 23, 2025, as indicated below.

Under the California Public Records Act (CPRA) you are entitled to copies of identifiable, non-exempt public records (Govt. Code section 7922.500). Please note that the CPRA requires the City to provide access to, or copies of, records responsive to your request which are in its possession, subject to certain exceptions. The CPRA does not require the City to provide information, answer questions, or create records which do not exist.

Records Requested:
“There is a solar panel powering a Flock ALPR camera located at Lat/Long 36.9677, -122.0186, the Northeast corner of 3rd Street and Riverside Ave in Santa Cruz, California. On that solar panel is a sticker that reads: "Property of NCRIC / NCHIDTA", "Flock Solar Panel", "10682". Please provide all contracts, MSAs, MOUs, NDAs, notes, emails and other documents associated with that solar panel.”

The City has extended its time to respond to your request to ­Monday, January 26, 2026 pursuant to the California Public Records Act, Government section 7920.000 et seq. (the “CPRA”), for the following reason:

☒ Government Code § 7922.535(c)(2) • The need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request.

We appreciate your patience while we thoroughly process your request. Please do not hesitate to contact us with any questions.

Thank you,

Brittany Inclan
Records Coordinator
City of Santa Cruz | City Manager’s Office | City Clerk’s Division
809 Center Street, Santa Cruz, CA 95060
Direct: 831-420-5034
Email: binclan@santacruzca.gov
Web: santacruzca.gov<https://www.cityofsantacruz.com/>
[cid:image001.png@01DC83E6.34828D00]

Public Records Act Requests may be submitted online via the Public Records Request form or by hard copy form available at the City Clerk’s Office located at 809 Center Street, Room 8, Santa Cruz, CA 95060.

Please note: Public Record Act Requests submitted via email, fax, USPS, or drop off after 5:00 p.m. on a business day, Saturdays, Sundays, or holidays will be processed as received on the next open business day. The 10-day response period begins when the request is received.

(CA. Gov't Code §7920.000 et seq.)


Re: PUBLIC RECORDS ACT REQUEST RECEIVED ON ­Tuesday, December 23, 2025

The City of Santa Cruz responds to your public records request for information received on ­Tuesday, December 23, 2025, as indicated below.

I hereby request the following records: There is a solar panel powering a Flock ALPR camera located at Lat/Long 36.9677, -122.0186, the Northeast corner of 3rd Street and Riverside Ave in Santa Cruz, California. Under the California Public Records Act (CPRA) you are entitled to copies of identifiable, non-exempt public records (Govt. Code section 7922.530). Julia Wood

Email: jwood@santacruzca.gov<mailto:jwood@santacruzca.gov>

[cid:image001.png@01DCBDFF.31B15E50]

Public Records Requests may be submitted online via the Public Records Request form or by hard copy form available at the City Clerk's Office located at 809 Center Street, Room 8, Santa Cruz, CA 95060.

Please note: Public Record Act Requests submitted via email, fax, USPS, or drop off after 5:00 p.m. on a business day, Saturdays, Sundays, or holidays will be processed as received on the next open business day. The 10-day response period begins when the request is received.

title: SNAP status

communications.subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: SNAP status
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

All official communications — including but not limited to emails, memos, letters, text messages, meeting notes, and internal reports — concerning changes to Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) as a result of H.R.1 - One Big Beautiful Bill Act signed on July 4, 2025 from July 4, 2025 to the date of this record request. This request specifically includes communications relating to:
-Policy changes
-Program administration or implementation
-Eligibility determinations or rule changes
-Oversight, audits, or funding of SNAP
-Communications with federal agencies or contractors regarding SNAP

If feasible, please include communications sent to or from:
-Officials or staff within the Arizona Department of Economic Security
-Arizona DES Director Michael Wisehart
-SNAP Division leadership or staff
-Any relevant communications between IDHS and the USDA Food and Nutrition Service (FNS)

If these records exist in electronic format, I prefer to receive them electronically via email or download link. Please provide the records in searchable PDF format if possible.
If this request will result in charges, please provide a cost estimate before fulfilling it. 

If you determine that any portion of this request is exempt from disclosure, please provide a written explanation citing the specific exemption(s) and inform me of my rights to appeal.
Thank you for your attention to this request. I look forward to your response within the ten business days required by law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samantha Liss

communications.subject: Department Records Request :: D001614-092925
communications.communication:
Dear Samantha Liss: 
Thank you for contacting the Arizona Department of Economic Security (DES).  DES acknowledges receipt of your Public Records Request.  Your request was received in this office on 9/29/2025 and given the reference number D001614-092925 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

All official communications — including but not limited to emails, memos, letters, text messages, meeting notes, and internal reports — concerning changes to Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) as a result of H.R.1 - One Big Beautiful Bill Act signed on July 4, 2025 from July 4, 2025 to the date of this record request. This request specifically includes communications relating to:
-Policy changes
-Program administration or implementation
-Eligibility determinations or rule changes
-Oversight, audits, or funding of SNAP
-Communications with federal agencies or contractors regarding SNAP

If feasible, please include communications sent to or from:
-Officials or staff within the Arizona Department of Economic Security
-Arizona DES Director Michael Wisehart
-SNAP Division leadership or staff
-Any relevant communications between IDHS and the USDA Food and Nutrition Service (FNS)

If these records exist in electronic format, I prefer to receive them electronically via email or download link. Please provide the records in searchable PDF format if possible.
If this request will result in charges, please provide a cost estimate before fulfilling it.

If you determine that any portion of this request is exempt from disclosure, please provide a written explanation citing the specific exemption(s) and inform me of my rights to appeal.
Thank you for your attention to this request. I look forward to your response within the ten business days required by law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samantha Liss

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fdepartment-of-economic-security-5983%252Fsnap-status-193810%252F%253Femail%253DPublicRecordsRequest%252540azdes.gov&url_auth_token=AAATdPDAEJsvWHFj7Zne5sHm52c%3A1v3Rkr%3Ao0DxBTY4akrIPm6TaYwfckyWAL-h6sF0_S53yos3Okk 
We will begin processing your request the next business day when received after normal business hours or on a weekend or holiday.  Upon review of your request, DES may email you if clarification is needed to ensure we are gathering the correct records for you. 
If this request is for commercial purposes or if the request is for electronic records to be printed or provided via CD/DVD, processing fees may apply.  You will be notified via email of any processing fees associated with your request, along with the ways in which the fees can be paid.  DES does not charge applicants or recipients of DES services for copies of their program records. 
PLEASE NOTE: Arizona law does not require DES to create records that do not already exist or are not regularly generated, including tallying and compiling aggregate information. The law also does not require DES to produce an electronic record in a format other than the existing format. 
To view the details or contact DES regarding this request please log into your My Records Center on the DES Public Records Request Portal. You will receive an email when your request has been completed. 
Thank you for using the DES Public Records Request Portal.

Samantha Liss The record(s) responsive to your request are now available in your My Request Center.  Please log into the Public Records Request Portal using the link below to retrieve the responsive records. For your convenience, a selection of relevant policies were pulled from the publicly available CNAP manual and provided to you in your My Request Center. However, all policies (with links to supporting documentation) are available at: https://dbmefaapolicy.azdes.gov/ . Additionally, this manual contains a “What’s Changed” section where you can view revisions to the policy manual. If you are interested in additional records, please submit a new request to the Public Records Request Portal . Importantly, please describe the record(s) requested in sufficient detail and specificity to allow the record to be located with a reasonable amount of diligence. Important: Files can only be viewed once they are downloaded and will only be available for download for 30 days. After 30 days from the date the records are released, you will no longer be able to download or view them and must submit a new request before records can be re-released. Department Records Request - D001614-092925 In the event DES has produced any document containing unredacted confidential or privileged information, DES hereby asserts that such disclosure was accidental and does not waive any protections afforded under the law. If you encounter any accidental disclosures, please immediately delete the document with the unredacted confidential information and inform DES of the error. DES will return a properly redacted copy to you. Sincerely, DES Public Records Team

Sophie Choong

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Vermont Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

* Email messages and message contents with the keywords "RTO" or "Return to Office" in the email message subject or message body sent or received by Jason Maulucci, Director of Policy Development & Legislative Affairs, inclusive of 2024, 2025, and 2026 to date.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brandon Galbraith

communications.subject: RE: Vermont Public Records Act Request: State of Vermont Governor's Office, Jason Maulucci, Director of Policy Dev & Legislative Affairs, Return to Office Communications

I wanted to follow up on the following Vermont Public Records Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on March 23, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

communications.subject: RE: Vermont Public Records Act Request: State of Vermont Governor's Office, Jason Maulucci, Director of Policy Dev & Legislative Affairs, Return to Office Communications
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon Mr. Galbraith

This email is to confirm receipt of your request below. Please know that we are making a good faith effort to complete your request as timely as possible and I expect to have it completed this afternoon or tomorrow.

Thank you for your patience.
Karen

communications.subject: RE: Vermont Public Records Act Request: State of Vermont Governor's Office, Jason Maulucci, Director of Policy Dev & Legislative Affairs, Return to Office Communications
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon Mr. Galbraith -

In response to your request below, except as set forth herein, attached please find records responsive to your request.

Certain personal information has been redacted to the extent it is exempt from disclosure in accordance with 1 V.S.A. § 315(a) as a matter of respecting personal privacy.


Also, certain records have been withheld to the extent they are exempt from disclosure under 1 V.S.A. § 317(c)(4) as executive privileged. This includes weekly and legislative reports advisory to the Governor and policy and strategy materials on matters relating to return to office.

Jaye Pershing Johnson, the Governor's Legal Counsel, is responsible for denial of the privileged and confidential records. I conferred with Ms. Johnson when making the determination to redact or withhold information.

As the records custodian, I certify, as required by 1 V.S.A. 318(a)(2), that I consider the redacted and withheld materials to be exempt from disclosure. You have the right to appeal, as explained in 1 V.S.A. 318, to Jason Gibbs, Chief of Staff, relative to my certification of the records as exempt.

Sincerely,
Karen Pallas
Records Officer

title: Ross RIPA requests

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Ross RIPA requests
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Any and all records reflecting data collected and recorded by the Police Department in accordance with the California Racial and Identity Profiling Act (RIPA) from January 1, 2025, through the present. Please provide the raw data in excel or csv format.

2) Any and all reports, records, and analyses created by, for, or at the direction of, the City/Town that pertain to any RIPA data that has been collected by the Police Department since January 1, 2025.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tammy Edmonson

title: Request on Political Appointees (Federal Communications Commission)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request on Political Appointees (Federal Communications Commission)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act. I hereby request the following records:

1. A list of any and all political appointments at the agency appointed from January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request.

2. Copies of resumes for any and all political appointees hired since January 20, 2025 until the date of your final response to this request. These records are likely in the possession of the Designated Agency Ethics Official (DAEO).

Please provide these records as available on a rolling basis.

Documented is an investigative watchdog and reporting group that explores the relationship between special interests and government. This request is likely to contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of the operations of the government. Disclosure of the requested records serves the public interest, and this request is not being made for any commercial purposes. Therefore, I am requesting a waiver of any fees. If you do not grant my fee waiver request, please notify me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request be filled electronically by email attachment or other electronic file transfer if available, or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.  I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,
Nina

title: Records Request (Waterbury Town Clerk)

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Request (Waterbury Town Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to [the Freedom of Information Act / applicable state public records law], I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera / school bus infraction detection system associated with: 

Solicitation/Bid: RFP # 8320

Title/Description: Automated Traffic Enforcement System 

Issuing Entity: The City of Waterbury Police Department

Specifically, I am requesting: 

All proposals and responses submitted by all vendors. 

All contracts, agreements, amendments, addenda, and pricing schedules resulting from this solicitation. 

All evaluation materials, including scoring sheets, evaluation committee notes or summaries, and recommendation memoranda. 

All bid tabulations, comparison matrices, or award justifications. 

All addenda to the solicitation, and any questions and answers (Q&A) or clarifications issued to vendors. 

Any other documentation or correspondence (including emails, letters, or internal memoranda) material to the evaluation, selection, and award of this stop-arm camera enforcement program. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your acknowledgment.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jon Smith

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

1 - List of all Placement Partners, including their contact information (name, phone, email), and status (Active, Inactive, Terminated, Suspended). 

2 - Records related to the criteria, qualifications and documentation required for organizations to become Placement Partners.

3 - Records related to the number of animals transferred to each Placement Partners in the past year.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Colon

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2229618/

Dear Jonathan Colon

Please allow this to acknowledge this office's receipt of your request, referenced above, under the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL).

Be advised that a determination to grant or deny this request will be provided to you within 20 business days of the date of this acknowledgment.  If this request is granted, we will make every effort to grant access to the records within those 20 business days, however, if circumstances prevent disclosure within the 20 business days, you will be contacted in writing with the reason for the delay and provided a date by which the records will be made available.  Moreover, we will inform you of the costs (.25 per standard page) associated with the copying of any documents subject to FOIL.

title: NCAA Financial Report (Longwood University)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: NCAA Financial Report (Longwood University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern,

I request the NCAA Membership Financial Reporting System revenue and expense reports for fiscal (or reporting) years 2004, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, 2009 & 2024.  Specifically:  

These reports are completed by the athletic department for the National Collegiate Athletic Association for the fiscal years listed above. This report is a multi-page document that had to be submitted to the NCAA in January. It contains 30+ revenue and expense categories, followed by specific breakdowns of each of those categories, by sport and gender. I am requesting the full report, including the detail tables, the “Other Reporting Items” list and the “Statement of Revenues and Expenses” that appears at the end of the report. PLEASE NOTE: The NCAA report is different than the equity report that is sent to the U.S. Department of Education for Title IX compliance.

If your department does not maintain these public records, please let me know who does and include the proper custodian’s name and address.

I prefer to receive the records electronically as an Adobe Acrobat (.pdf) file.

If you choose to deny this request, please provide a written explanation for the denial including a reference to the specific statutory exemption(s) on which you rely.  Also, please provide all segregable portions of otherwise exempt material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Stacey Brook.

title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Longwood University)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Longwood University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: NY DOT: Individual Inspection Records

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: NY DOT: Individual Inspection Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

I request individual-level inspection records for all inspections conducted by NYSDOT's Passenger Carrier Safety Bureau and Motor Carrier Compliance Bureau for the past 5 years (2021-2026), including:

Inspection date
Operator / carrier name and Operator ID
Vehicle identification (VIN, plate number, vehicle type)
Inspection location (facility, roadside, or terminal)
Inspection result (pass / fail)
Deficiencies found (if any) with category and description
Out-of-service orders issued (if any)
Inspector ID or facility ID
Re-inspection date and result (if applicable)

I understand this is a large dataset. I am requesting a structured database export in CSV or pipe-delimited format. If the full 5-year span is too large for a single response, I am willing to receive the data in annual increments.

Note: I am aware that aggregated versions of this data are published via the OSIPD API and BUSNET dataset on data.ny.gov. I am specifically requesting the underlying individual inspection records that feed those aggregations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ian White

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Ian White

Inspection date

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Ian White
