communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request #R010047-021626
communications.communication:
Thank you for the responsive records received today. The Carrier Certification System data (active, suspended and revoked authority) and the individual inspection records (R010047, 11 files covering January 2021 through February 2026) are received and acknowledged.                                

I have one clarification on the inspection records. The R010047 files contain bus and passenger vehicle inspections conducted at operator facilities. Each record joins to an Operator Number assigned to passenger carriers (charter, common, contract). No equivalent inspection records for truck or freight carriers appear in this delivery. 

Could you confirm whether NYSDOT maintains a similar inspection dataset for property/freight carriers? If so, I request those records under the same FOIL request. If NYSDOT does not conduct facility-level inspections for freight    carriers (as distinct from FMCSA roadside inspections), a brief confirmation of that would close this item.

All other responsive records in this delivery appear complete. Thank you for  the prompt response!

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request #R010047-021626
communications.communication:
After inspecting the files, I was hoping you could clarify the data:

2025 inspection data appears truncated. The R010047 files for both halves of 2025 contain exactly 100,000 rows each. The H1 file ends on 24-06-2025 (missing 25-30 June) and the H2 file ends on 22-12-2025 (missing 23-31 December). Prior years range from 79,000 to 90,000 rows per half-year, which suggests a row limit was applied during export. Could you re-export both 2025 files without the 100,000-row cap?

Thank you again for your responsiveness.

communications.subject: New Message :: R010047-021626
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Dear Ian White, 
On 3/18/2026 11:36 you wrote: 
"Thank you for the responsive records received today. The Carrier Certification System data (active, suspended and revoked authority) and the individual inspection records (R010047, 11 files covering January 2021 through February 2026) are received and acknowledged. I have one clarification on the inspection records. The R010047 files contain bus and passenger vehicle inspections conducted at operator facilities. Each record joins to an Operator Number assigned to passenger carriers (charter, common, contract). No equivalent inspection records for truck or freight carriers appear in this delivery. Could you confirm whether NYSDOT maintains a similar inspection dataset for property/freight carriers? If so, I request those records under the same FOIL request. If NYSDOT does not conduct facility-level inspections for freight carriers (as distinct from FMCSA roadside inspections), a brief confirmation of that would close this item. All other responsive records in this delivery appear complete. Thank you for the prompt response! After inspecting the files, I was hoping you could clarify the data: 2025 inspection data appears truncated. The R010047 files for both halves of 2025 contain exactly 100, 000 rows each. The H1 file ends on 24-06-2025 (missing 25-30 June) and the H2 file ends on 22-12-2025 (missing 23-31 December). Prior years range from 79, 000 to 90, 000 rows per half-year, which suggests a row limit was applied during export. Could you re-export both 2025 files without the 100, 000-row cap? Thank you again for your responsiveness. Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2244206/" 
With respect to the portion of your inquiry asking about " a similar inspection dataset for property/freight carriers", a diligent search of our files failed to reveal any records that are responsive to your request.  To the extent that you are also seeking a statement of information regarding our agency's functions, FOIL is specific to access of agency records and requests for information, answers to questions, or explanations of records provided are not contemplated by the law.  The Committee on Open Government, the New York State agency which provides advisory opinions on FOIL matters, has stated in opinion FOIL-AO-13208, “I note that the title of the [F]reedom of Information Law may be somewhat misleading, for it is not a vehicle that requires agencies to provide information per se; rather, it requires agencies to disclose records to the extent provided by law.” 
With respect to the portion of your inquiry seeking a " re-export both 2025 files without the 100,000-row cap", thank you for making me aware of this specific issue. I have reached out to the program area to request that they repull the 2025 files with an eye towards assuring that the data is not limited by a row cap.  I will release those records to you once the program area has made them available to me. Your records will be released via this, our Records Access Center -- we will not upload documents to an external website. 
Thank you kindly, 
Jonathan Allen 
Records Access Officer 
The New York State Department of Transportation

title: Request to Marines for "Big Rocks Meeting" Communication Director briefs, from June 2025 to present

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Marines for "Big Rocks Meeting" Communication Director briefs, from June 2025 to present
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All "Big Rocks Meeting" Communication Directorate briefs of the US Marines from June 11, 2025 to the present. The Marines previously released to me, via FOIA, a collection of such briefs dated from January 2022 until June 10, 2025.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: Your FOIA Request 2026-001850
communications.communication:
Mr. Carollo - This is to acknowledge the receipt of your requests and to let you know that I am the analyst assigned for processing.  If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me or our office. 

Please be aware that due to the government shutdown we are still ‘catching up’ and there may be some significant delays in receiving a response.  Please note that we are doing our best and are working diligently to complete all incoming requests. 

Please also be aware that, whenever a FOIA requester has not received a determination within 20 working days from the date a request is perfected, they are entitled to challenge the delay.  Appeals may be submitted within the SecureRelease online portal at www.securerelease.us or by writing to the Department of the Navy, Office of the Judge Advocate General (Code 14), 1322 Patterson Ave SE, Suite 3000, Washington Navy Yard, DC 20374-5066.  If you prefer, you can also submit an appeal via e-mail to OJAG-C14-FOIA-PA-ADMINAPPEALS@us.navy.mil.  Your appeal, if any, should include a statement indicating why you believe it should be granted. 

You also have the right to seek assistance and/or dispute resolution services from the Department of the Navy FOIA Public Liaison, Mr. Christopher Julka, at (703) 697-0031 or christopher.a.julka.civ@us.navy.mil.  You may also contact the Office of Government Information Services for assistance at ogis@nara.gov or (877) 684-6448. 

Sincerely, 

Deanna James 
FOIA Analyst

title: Epstein Investigation (Federal Bureau of Investigation)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Epstein Investigation (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552), I request access to all non-exempt records, documents, and evidence in your possession relating to investigations of Jeffrey Edward Epstein (DOB: January 20, 1953), including but not limited to:

1. Flight logs from Epstein’s private aircraft (including aircraft tail numbers N908JE, N212JE, and N474AA), dated from January 1, 1995, to August 10, 2019.


2. Inventories and logs of physical and digital evidence seized during law enforcement raids of the following Epstein properties:

9 East 71st Street, Manhattan, NY

Little St. James Island, U.S. Virgin Islands

Palm Beach mansion, FL

Zorro Ranch, NM



3. Any itemized lists, reports, or media inventories documenting the contents of:

CDs, hard drives, or other storage devices labeled or cataloged by law enforcement

Surveillance footage recorded inside Epstein's residences or private aircraft



4. Chain-of-custody documentation for any seized videos, digital drives, or photographic evidence.


5. Correspondence between the FBI and the Department of Justice or other government agencies regarding the management, review, or disposition of the aforementioned evidence.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Justin Griggs

title: Response to Medical Emergencies - Florida Department of Corrections

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Response to Medical Emergencies - Florida Department of Corrections
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

All written policies and procedures of the Florida Department of Corrections governing responses to medical emergencies, including policies related to the transport of incarcerated individuals to community hospitals or other external medical facilities.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Sophie Choong

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Hopkinton School District)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Hopkinton School District)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: Re: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Hopkinton School District)
communications.communication:
Hello,

We are in receipt of your RTK request and have forwarded it to the
appropriate department for processing. We will be in touch with you within
5 business days as to when those documents will be available as prescribed
by law (see below).



RSA 91-A:2, IV - Each public body or agency shall, upon request for any
governmental record reasonably described, make available for inspection and
copying any such governmental record within its files when such records are
immediately available for such release. If a public body or agency is
unable to make a governmental record available for immediate inspection and
copying, it shall, within 5 business days of request, make such record
available, deny the request in writing with reasons, or furnish written
acknowledgment of the receipt of the request and a statement of the time
reasonably necessary to determine whether the request shall be granted or
denied.


Thank you,
Jodi Condon
Executive Assistant to the Superintendent



-- 
Thank you,
Jodi Condon
Executive Assistant to the Superintendent and Business Administrator
Hopkinton School District

title: Ritchie County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Ritchie County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Kierstyn Aubrecht
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 20, 2026

115 E Main St, Harrisville, WV 26362                                                                                                        

To Whom it may concern,
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Ritchie County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 

The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely,
Kierstyn Aubrecht
kra00019@mix.wvu.edu

title: Request for records concerning Peregrine Technologies, Nightingale Security DFR program, and BRINC Responder UAS integration

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Request for records concerning Peregrine Technologies, Nightingale Security DFR program, and BRINC Responder UAS integration
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request electronic copies of all records, communications, and attachments from January 1, 2018 to present concerning the Peregrine Technologies data-integration / analytics platform and its integration with other surveillance or first-responder systems used by the San Pablo Police Department (SPPD).

1. Contracts & Procurements
All contracts, amendments, renewals, purchase orders, invoices, or memoranda between the City / SPPD and Peregrine Technologies, including those referenced in Resolution 2021-060 and the Dec 2023 Year-End Brochure listing a $100,000 license (12/18/22 – 12/17/23).
All contracts, amendments, or staff reports for Resolution 2023-110 (three-year Nightingale Security DFR agreement, $644,311) and File #24-410 (BRINC Responder Operations Bundle purchase).
Any Statements of Work, proposals, or quotes listing Peregrine modules or features such as video analytics, real-time insights, resource-allocation tools, dashboards, or heat-mapping.

2. Technical Integrations & System Design
Documents describing interfaces, APIs, or data feeds connecting Peregrine with: ALPR systems; ShotSpotter / SoundThinking; CCTV / situational cameras; body-worn camera systems; CAD / RMS databases; and Nightingale or BRINC drone video feeds.
Configuration manuals or network diagrams showing where data flows between these systems.

3. Analytics Logic & “Insights”
Any documentation, policy, or training material defining how Peregrine produces “insights,” “video analytics,” or “real-time decision” outputs.
Records describing rules, thresholds, weighting, or triggers used for alerts, heat maps, or resource-placement recommendations.
User guides or presentations provided to SPPD officers or analysts explaining how to interpret or act on Peregrine insights.

4. Bias Testing / Auditing / Impact Assessments
Reports, audits, or internal reviews evaluating accuracy, false-positive rates, or disparate impact of Peregrine analytics, ALPR hits, or DFR dispatches.
Data showing alert, incident, or DFR-launch counts by neighborhood or census tract (from 2019 to present).
Correspondence with Peregrine, Nightingale, or BRINC discussing equity, bias, fairness, or community impact.

5. Data Retention & Sharing
Current and prior data-retention schedules for Peregrine-related data and any data-sharing agreements with vendors or partner agencies.
Clauses or memos describing vendor access rights to SPPD data for analytics, testing, or model training.

6. Public Relations & Performance Claims
Talking points, press releases, or communications referencing Peregrine’s role in crime reduction (e.g., claims of an “80 percent crime reduction” or use of “innovative AI tools,” as reported in Police1 articles dated Dec 18 2024 and Apr 10 2025).
Any presentations, vendor demos, or community-outreach slides referencing “AI,” “video analytics,” “insights,” or “real-time decision making.”

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Heather Gilligan

title: FOIA - Michael A. Taylor Incident

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA - Michael A. Taylor Incident
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

An electronic copy of the bodycam footage regarding the Michael A. Taylor incident that occurred on October 22, 2024 after a traffic stop at the Dollar General located at 914 Sheridan Rd, Winthrop Harbor, IL 60096. Taylor was arrested on the charges of bribery, aggravated battery to a peace officer and resisting a peace officer causing injury.

Specifically, I am seeking to obtain electronic copies of the:

1. BWC footage recorded by Winthrop Harbor Police Department's responding officers (main officer and assisting) detailing their full involvement in this incident

To assist you in locating the footage here are the key details of the incident:
Date of Event: October 22, 2024
Names: Michael A. Taylor
Location of Event: 914 Sheridan Rd, Winthrop Harbor, IL 60096

Please let me know if you require further information to process it.

If any aspects of this request are denied, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and please notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Johnson

title: Employee Communications Records - Wallingford Public Schools

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Employee Communications Records - Wallingford Public Schools
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act (C.G.S. § 1-200 et seq.), I respectfully request access to the following public records:

PRIMARY RECORDS REQUESTED:
- All electronic communications (emails, text messages, instant messages, social media communications) sent or received by Lisette Giacobbe during her employment from January 1, 2024 to present
- All communications between school district employees containing the names "Conrad," "John Conrad," "Emelia Conrad," or "Sage Conrad" from January 1, 2024 to present
- Complete personnel file for Lisette Giacobbe including any disciplinary actions, complaints, performance evaluations, or incident reports

COMPREHENSIVE DISCLOSURE REQUIREMENTS:
This request includes, but is not limited to:
- All responsive records regardless of format (paper, electronic, audio, video, social media, messaging apps)
- Records stored on personal devices if used for official business
- Records in possession of contractors, consultants, or third parties acting on behalf of the agency
- Draft documents, notes, calendars, and informal communications
- Records that have been deleted but remain recoverable
- Metadata and audit logs associated with electronic records

SEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
- Search all relevant custodians, not just those specifically named
- Search all email accounts, shared drives, and backup systems
- Include records of employees no longer with the agency
- Search alternative spellings, nicknames, and partial names

PRODUCTION STANDARDS:
- Provide records in native electronic format when possible
- Do not convert searchable documents to non-searchable formats
- Preserve all metadata including timestamps and sender/recipient information
- Provide a privilege log for any claimed exemptions

If any responsive records have been destroyed, please provide:
- Documentation of the destruction
- Reason for destruction
- Date of destruction
- Authority for destruction

If you claim any exemption under the Freedom of Information Act, please:
- Identify the specific statutory exemption
- Explain how the exemption applies to each document
- Provide redacted copies where partial disclosure is possible
- Consider whether public interest outweighs any claimed exemption

This request seeks records that serve the public interest in government transparency and accountability. Please construe all exemptions narrowly and err on the side of disclosure.

Please preserve all potentially responsive records immediately upon receipt of this request to prevent spoliation.

As these records serve significant public interest, please waive all fees. If fees cannot be waived, please provide detailed cost estimate before proceeding.

Thank you for your assistance.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

john Conrad

title: Cozen invoices

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Cozen invoices
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Since January 1, 2025 through current:

1) Copies of all invoices from the Cozen O'Connor law firm;

2) Copies of records of payments by CPS/Chicago Board of Education to Cozen O'Connor law firm. I want the actual bills as well as proof of payment by the district.

3) Any and all letters of engagement, if changed from previous engagement letters, between CPS/Chicago Board of Education and Cozen O'Connor law firm, including any modifications or changes.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2238490/ 
 
Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center. 

Track the issue status and respond at: https://cps.mycusthelp.com/WEBAPP//_rs/RequestEdit.aspx?rid=19224

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2238490/ RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please note that portions of these records were redacted to shield material covered by attorney-client privilege and work-product privilege pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(m) which exempts communications between a public body and an attorney representing the public body that would not be subject to discovery in litigation,  and materials prepared or compiled by or for a public body in anticipation of a criminal,  civil or administrative proceeding upon the request of an attorney advising the public body. Redactions have been made pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(kk) which exempts the public body's credit card numbers, debit card numbers, bank account numbers, Federal Employer Identification Number, security code numbers, passwords, and similar account information, the disclosure of which could result in identity theft or impression or defrauding of a governmental entity or a person. You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Butte County Sheriff)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Butte County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Salvatore Prescott

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Salvatore Prescott
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Since January 1, 2025 through current:

1) Copies of all invoices from the Salvatore Prescott law firm;

2) Copies of records of payments by CPS/Chicago Board of Education to Salvatore Prescott law firm. I want the actual bills as well as proof of payment by the district.

3) Any and all letters of engagement, if changed from previous engagement letters, between CPS/Chicago Board of Education and Salvatore Prescott law firm, including any modifications or changes.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2238491/ 
 
Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center. 

Track the issue status and respond at: https://cps.mycusthelp.com/WEBAPP//_rs/RequestEdit.aspx?rid=19225

03/26/2026 Miriam Bhimani 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019225-031126 Thank you for using the Chicago Public Schools FOIA Center. You requested the following: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

Since January 1, 2025 through current: 

1) Copies of all invoices from the Salvatore Prescott law firm; 

2) Copies of records of payments by CPS/Chicago Board of Education to Salvatore Prescott law firm. I want the actual bills as well as proof of payment by the district. 

3) Any and all letters of engagement, if changed from previous engagement letters, between CPS/Chicago Board of Education and Salvatore Prescott law firm, including any modifications or changes. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2238491/ RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please note that certain private information (signatures) was redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7. Specifically, section 7(1)(b) which exempts certain private information as defined by section 2(c-5) and section 7(1)(c) which exempts personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subject of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs any legitimate public interest in obtaining the information Docusign ID Codes were redacted pursuant to (5 ILCS 140/ 7(1)(u), which exempts information that would disclose or might lead to the disclosure of secret or confidential information, codes, algorithms, programs, or private keys intended to be used to create electronic or digital signatures under the Electronic Commerce Security Act. Payment links were redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(kk) which exempts the public body's credit card numbers, debit card numbers, bank account numbers, Federal Employer Identification Number, security code numbers, passwords, and similar account information, the disclosure of which could result in identity theft or impression or defrauding of a governmental entity or a person. 
You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

communications.subject: [NYS OAG FOIL Request Center] General FOIL Request :: G000757-110524
communications.communication:
March 26, 2026 

RE: Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) Request Reference # G000757-110524 

Dear Sam Mellins: 
This email is in response to the above-­referenced FOIL request. 
The Office of the Attorney General has conducted a diligent search for the records that you have requested. 
Please access this request in the NYS OAG FOIL Request Center to retrieve records that respond to your request. 

Please be advised that some of the records that respond to your request have been withheld in their entirety, or portions have been redacted and appear as blacked out in the documents produced to you.  These records, or portions of records, are exempt from disclosure pursuant to: 

• Public Officers Law § 87(2)(a), which provides that records that are exempted from disclosure by state or federal statute are exempt from disclosure under FOIL.  The records constitute attorney work product, which is exempt from disclosure under Civil Practice Law and Rules § 3101(c); and 

• Public Officers Law § 87(2)(b), because their disclosure would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. 

You have a right to appeal the foregoing decision.  If you elect to do so, you must appeal in writing, within 30 days, to Kathryn Sheingold, Records Appeals Officer.  Your appeal can be sent by email to FOIL.Appeals@ag.ny.gov or by mail to State of New York, Office of the Attorney General, Division of Appeals and Opinions, The Capitol, Albany, New York 12224.  You can reach the Records Appeals Officer by telephone at (518) 776-2009. 

This is our eighth and final response to the above-referenced request. 

Accordingly, this FOIL request is now closed. 

Sincerely, 

Records Access Officer 
 
Please visit the NYS OAG FOIL Request Center to view frequently asked questions, monitor request progress, and submit future requests. 
 View this request in the My Request Center.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Carson City Sheriff’s Department)

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Carson City Sheriff’s Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Internal Investigation Reports naming Nicholas Cassalia
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

The Colorado Springs Police Department (“CSPD”) has received your records request dated 2/1/2026, and it is under review. The records you have requested are considered criminal justice records as defined in Colorado Revised Statute § 24-72-302(4) which are held in the sole possession of CSPD, a law enforcement agency.  The Colorado Criminal Justice Records Act (“CCJRA”) as codified in C.R.S. § 24-72-301 et. seq. governs the release of criminal justice records.  The time response deadlines contained within CORA do not apply to requests for criminal justice records governed by the CCJRA.

Kind Regards,

Internal Affairs Section
Colorado Springs Police Department
719-444-7417 (office)

title: Marines support for National Geographic TV documentary series "Top Guns"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Marines support for National Geographic TV documentary series "Top Guns"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Marine Corps and the production of the National Geographic TV documentary series "Top Guns: The Next Generation" (2025-present). I request any and all documents pertaining to Marine Corps support for "Top Guns," including but not not limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the Marines and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Top Guns," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation. These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Santa Cruz County Elections Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Santa Cruz County Elections Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Cast vote record (CVR) data for the following elections:
1. 2022 Nov Midterm Election
2. 2024 Nov General Election

Specifically, for each of these elections, I’m looking for the full anonymized record of votes cast generated by the relevant voting system technology in your County in that year. In case it is helpful to see an example of the type of data I am requesting from your county, Alameda County has these records posted for the 2024 General Election on their Election Information for November 2024 under the name “Cast Vote Records (CVR) Report”.

Please let me know if you need any further clarification on the type of data that I am requesting.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aleksandra Conevska

title: Any emails containing the words "60 Minutes"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Any emails containing the words "60 Minutes"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any emails that contain the words "60 Minutes" sent by the following list of people:

- Commissioner Brendan Carr
- Chief of Staff Greg Watson
- Legal Advisor  Danielle Thumann
- Legal Advisor Arpan A. Sura
- Assistant Drema Johnson

The records can be retrieved by a keyword search email subjects, recipients and message bodies from Jan. 1, 2025 to the date of the search

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ben Welsh

title: Pendleton, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Pendleton, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Kylie Frederick
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
Mar. 12 2026

Elise Miller White, County Clerk
100 South Main Street, P.O. Box 1167
Franklin, WV 26807

Dear, Elise Miller White
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Pendleton County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting:
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes.
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.
Thank you for considering my request.
Sincerely, Kylie Frederick
kyliejfrederick04@gmail.com

communications.subject: RE: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Pendleton, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Ms. Frederick,

I have reviewed your request and am working on gathering the requested documents. I anticipate having everything by Friday, March 20, 2026.

Thank you.

I apologize for not getting the information you requested to you by Friday as I had originally indicated. I came down with bronchitis and was off work all last week. I am now back at work and will try to get the information for you over the next few days.

Elise

communications.subject: RE: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Pendleton, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Kylie,

 

I have attached the information you requested regarding the Opioid funds received and spent. We do not use an application form for requests to received funds. Generally the organization will either request by mail or email to the County Administrator or one of our County Commissioners. I have attached a copy of our Revenue and Expenditure ledger showing funds we have received and disbursed. I have also attached copies of the minutes (relevant section highlighted) where the County Commission considered the requests for opioid funds. 

The procedure is as follows: a request is made for the funds, the Commission considers the request and either approves or disapproves the request. That information will be noted in the minutes. Our County Commissioners are Carl Hevener, President, Roger Dahmer and Jimmie Bennett.

If you require additional information or clarification on any of the above, please let me know.

Thank you.
